title: Navigation Contract Standard
summary: Reusable field contract for standards that declare how their artifact kind is read through native bands, scopes, facets, source authority, currentness, edge neighborhoods, and validation.
purpose: Turn the Kind Atlas from a list of artifact kinds into a ladder-aware map where each kind's governing standard or profile says how a row can be compressed, scoped, faceted, drilled, populated, and verified.

field_contracts.profile_id: Stable contract/profile id for this artifact kind's navigation ladder.
field_contracts.artifact_kind: Kind id from the Kind Atlas or a narrower sub-kind governed by the standard.
field_contracts.navigable_bands: Ordered density levels over the same conceptual object, cheapest to deepest. Band names are kind-native, not globally fixed.
field_contracts.band_contracts: Object keyed by band name. Each band declares purpose, minimum_payload, forbidden, and drilldown_to.
field_contracts.navigable_scopes: Structural zoom units for this kind, such as module, section, class, function, source_span, or term. Empty only when the artifact is truly scope-atomic.
field_contracts.navigable_facets: Aspect/section classes inside one scope, such as gap, code_loci, relationships, evidence, signature, body, or aliases.
field_contracts.telescope_facets: Compatibility alias from v0. New contracts should split entries into navigable_scopes and navigable_facets instead of adding mixed scope/facet terms here.
field_contracts.population_policy: Per emitted unit or selector, declares populated_by as authored, compiled, live_computed, or unpopulated, plus emitter/source/freshness details when known.
field_contracts.creator_skill_id: Skill or builder surface that produces or refreshes rows under this contract.
field_contracts.navigator_skill_id: Skill or kernel surface that reads/traverses rows under this contract.
field_contracts.source_authority: Where truth lives when a compressed row is insufficient.
field_contracts.currentness_policy: How the row exposes freshness, source fingerprints, generated-at posture, or live validation.
field_contracts.dependency_neighborhood_policy: How inbound/outbound edges, dependencies, or surrounding context are selected, downshifted, or omitted.
field_contracts.edge_compression_policy: Rules for compressing relationships as rows with source, target, verb, reverse verb, forward/reverse gloss, authority, evidence, drilldown, and decay posture.
field_contracts.validation_probe: Checks that tell whether a row or standard has satisfied the contract.

population_modes.authored: Written directly in the source artifact or curated registry row.
population_modes.compiled: Deterministically built from source into a generated projection and therefore freshness-bearing.
population_modes.live_computed: Derived at read time from live source without persisted projection authority.
population_modes.unpopulated: Contract exists but no emitter or populated content exists yet; navigators must report a profile gap.

edge_row_shape.rule: Every edge must explain why it matters from both ends and how much to trust the emitted relation. A one-way id link without confidence, reason, extraction mode, validity/currentness, and source evidence is not a navigable edge.

selected_node.rule: Read the requested row/scope/facet at the requested band.

first_order.rule: Expose direct inbound/outbound edges at card or context band when they affect the decision.

second_order.rule: Downshift neighbors-of-neighbors to flag unless the task explicitly needs deeper context.

beyond_second_order.rule: Omit with counts, reasons, and drilldown refs rather than silently dropping context.

standard_type_plane_profile.rule: A navigation contract is the per-type read ladder inside the standards type plane. It declares how a type is browsed, faceted, drilled, populated, and validated; it does not itself make a generated projection authoritative.
required_for_type_plane_support: artifact_kind matches a type_id or governed sub-kind
navigable_bands provide a cheap browse band before source authority opens
source_authority points at authored or generated authority with currentness policy
validation_probe proves the row still matches its standard and projection
edge_compression_policy names bidirectional gloss and evidence requirements
standard_type_plane_profile.handoff_to_kind_atlas: Kind Atlas reports support_status and profile_gap for each type; std_navigation_contract defines the shape of the missing or present profile.
standard_type_plane_profile.handoff_to_system_atlas: System Atlas may ingest the profile as derived_projection evidence, but mutation and contract changes route back to the governing standard.

validation_rules: navigable_bands must be ordered cheapest-to-deepest and every band must have a band_contracts entry.
Every band contract should include purpose, minimum_payload, forbidden, and drilldown_to.
navigable_scopes and navigable_facets must not conflate structural zoom with aspect/section selection.
population_policy must mark each declared emitted unit as authored, compiled, live_computed, or unpopulated.
Band names do not have to match flag/card/context/deep, but the first band must be cheap enough to browse a row set.
A declared navigation_contract does not imply an option-surface adapter exists. Adapter support remains reported by the Kind Atlas support_status.
If source_authority points at generated surfaces, currentness_policy must explain how source freshness is checked.
Edges that appear in card/context/evidence bands must carry bidirectional local glosses or be reported as thin_reverse_gloss/profile_gap.

Any compressed packet that emits a computed quality_signal bottleneck must preserve at least one active drilldown row for that bottleneck within the packet, or explicitly mark why no active row exists.

compliance_scanner_contract.purpose: Represent Kind Atlas navigation-contract coverage inside the shared compliance ledger without promoting drafts or changing option-surface support.

navigation_contract.rule: The audit may guide Atlas/navigation drilldowns after entry selected a navigation-contract lane; it must not become first-contact control flow or infer option-surface support from drafted contracts.

finding_policy.missing_contracts: warning aggregate finding with kind-id preview
finding_policy.incomplete_contracts: warning aggregate finding for discovered contracts that do not satisfy required_contract_fields
finding_policy.drafted_candidates: warning aggregate finding until promoted into source standards or profiles
finding_policy.audit_support_upgrade: error because the audit must preserve support status

finding_count_policy.purpose: Keep the scanner compact while preserving Atlas/navigation prioritization signal.
finding_count_policy.rule: A compact aggregate finding may represent many affected kinds; compliance cards must expose the affected count so Atlas does not under-rank the repair lane.

cluster_flag.purpose: Cluster-level browse signal for Prompt Shelf Metadata.

flag.purpose: One-row triage flag for a selected Prompt Shelf Metadata item.

card.purpose: Decision-ready card for a selected Prompt Shelf Metadata item.

band:cluster_flag.source: prompt shelf metadata and Type B to Type A handoff framing rows

band:flag.source: prompt shelf metadata and Type B to Type A handoff framing rows

band:card.source: prompt shelf metadata and Type B to Type A handoff framing rows

source_authority.projection_boundary: Prompt Shelf Metadata rows are navigable projections over their named source authorities; mutation routes stay with the standard or builder that owns the source row.

currentness_policy.rule: Use the option-surface row as browse projection only; when decisions require mutation or fresh truth, open the named source authority and run the validation probe.

dependency_neighborhood_policy.rule: Expose direct source authority, owner standard, builder, validation, and immediate semantic edges first; downshift broader system neighborhoods to counts with drilldown refs.

cluster_flag.purpose: Cluster-level browse over generated Microcosm task-class selectors grouped by accepted-organ family.

flag.purpose: One generated task-class selector row with primary organ, first command, evidence ref, receipt ref, and authority boundary.

card.purpose: Decision-ready route card for one task class, with relevant organs, generator checks, source refs, and scope limits.

band:cluster_flag.source: microcosm-substrate/atlas/agent_task_routes.json

band:flag.source: microcosm-substrate/atlas/agent_task_routes.json::routes

band:card.source: microcosm-substrate/atlas/agent_task_routes.json::routes[task_class=<task_class>]

source_authority.projection_boundary: Microcosm agent task route rows are generated selectors over accepted public organ cards; organ authority stays with each accepted organ's registry row, receipts, standard, paper module, and claim ceiling.

currentness_policy.rule: Use option-surface rows for navigation only. Before treating route content as fresh, run the organ-atlas owner check; before mutating route content, change upstream organ source rows and regenerate with the builder.

dependency_neighborhood_policy.rule: Expose task class, primary organ, relevant organs, first command, receipt refs, and source-relation summaries first; downshift full organ cards, source bodies, and runtime receipts to explicit drilldowns.

non_goals: Do not implement a generic row reader.
Do not implement a production telescope command from the standard alone.
Do not force one global band ladder across all artifact kinds.
Do not promote candidate contracts into support_status=option_surface_supported without an adapter and tests.
